headline: Voice-driven diagnostic conversation — debugging a stalled computer with mid-conversation pivots

text: Terry, my desktop is acting up. Fans spinning loud, nothing on the screen, won't respond to keyboard.
Yeah I can see it. Light on the case is solid white. Was working an hour ago.
Run me through a diagnostic order. Cheapest checks first.
Power cycle done — held the power button 10 seconds, unplugged for 30, plugged back in. Same thing. Fans loud, no display.
Monitor — could it be the monitor? Let me check.
Tried a different cable. Same. Tried the monitor on my laptop, monitor works fine. So it's not the monitor.
What's most likely now — GPU, RAM, or PSU?
How do I tell which one without opening the case yet?
Beep codes — does my motherboard have a speaker? Honestly I don't know.
It's an ASUS ROG Strix B650-E. Do those have onboard speakers or do you need to plug one in?
Be honest — do you actually know that model's spec or are you guessing?
Q-LED. OK what color is the Q-LED right now?
It's solid yellow. What does yellow mean on ASUS Q-LED?
RAM. So that's the failing component, or the cable to it, or a slot.
What's the order of things to try without buying anything new?
Reseat first. Walk me through it — I've never opened this case.
Hold on — actually, before I open it — is it safe? I'm on a carpet, no anti-static strap.
OK touch the case to ground myself, work on the desk. Got it. Side panel off.
Two RAM sticks, slot 2 and slot 4. I'll pull both.
Both reseated, click both sides. Power on.
Same. Yellow Q-LED, fans loud, no display.
Try one stick at a time?
Stick 1 only in slot 2 — boots! Display works.
So stick 2 is bad, or slot 4 is bad. How do I tell?
Right — put stick 2 in slot 2, stick 1 out. If it boots, slot 4 is the problem. If it doesn't, stick 2 is the problem.
Stick 2 in slot 2 — boots fine. So slot 4 on the motherboard is dead.
Now — actually, wait. Different question — could it be that the slot is fine but the stick wasn't seated? Should I retry slot 4?
Fair. Try stick 2 in slot 4 again, deliberate seat with both clicks.
Same — yellow Q-LED, no boot. Slot 4 is genuinely dead.
OK so I'm running on one stick of RAM. Performance hit?
Right — single channel instead of dual, ~10-20% memory bandwidth loss. Tolerable for now.
Save this as a vault note: 'B650-E motherboard - slot 4 dead - 2026-05-01'. Audience terry only. Tag 'hardware'.
Should I RMA the board, replace it, or live with it?
I'm 4 months past warranty. So pay for repair or replace. Ballpark cost of a B650-E replacement?
Be honest if you can't price it accurately. Just say it's roughly the original price minus depreciation.
OK plan: live with it for now, set a reminder to revisit in 3 months. Add calendar entry for Aug 1, 2026: 'review B650-E single-channel decision'.
Update the vault note with the plan: 'Living with single-channel for 3 months, then reassess.'
Done. Crisis averted. Thanks Terry.
